headline: Sci-Fi Survival Thriller 'Homewrecker' Assembles Powerhouse Female Cast Under Genre Master Xavier Gens
lede: Genre veteran Xavier Gens pivots from shark attacks to psychological survival with a provocative new sci-fi project starring Allison Williams and Michelle Randolph.
body:
## Rising Genre Talents Converge for High-Concept Thriller

Hollywood is preparing for another pulse-pounding sci-fi survival experience with Xavier Gens at the helm. The French filmmaker, fresh off Netflix's shark sensation 'Under Paris', is set to direct 'Homewrecker' — a project promising to blend psychological tension with speculative storytelling.

### Cast Signals Serious Genre Ambitions

Allison Williams, known for her transformative roles in 'M3GAN' and 'Get Out', brings substantial genre credibility to the project. Joining her is Michelle Randolph, emerging from the 'Scream' franchise with growing momentum in horror and thriller territories.

While plot details remain closely guarded, the project signals continued industry investment in female-led genre narratives that subvert traditional survival thriller conventions. Gens' track record with high-concept genre storytelling suggests 'Homewrecker' will deliver more than standard survival tropes.

### Industry Context

The film emerges during a renaissance of intelligent, female-driven sci-fi narratives that challenge audience expectations. Williams and Randolph represent a new generation of actors equally comfortable deconstructing genre expectations as inhabiting them.

Production details and release timeline are still taking shape, but the project already generates significant anticipation in independent film and genre circles.
key_facts: Xavier Gens directing after 'Under Paris' success
Allison Williams and Michelle Randolph co-starring
Sci-fi survival thriller with psychological elements
Project signals continued investment in female-led genre films
